title: Runaway Oven Temperature
shortDescription: The oven temperature has exceeded the maximum safe limit, indicating a potential runaway condition.
fullDescription: Error code F10 is a critical safety error indicating the oven temperature sensor (RTD) has detected a temperature exceeding the safe operating limit (typically above 650F during normal bake), or the control board suspects the temperature is rising uncontrollably. The oven immediately shuts off all heating elements.

causes.cause: Failed electronic oven control (EOC) board relay
Oven relay welded shut causing continuous heating

symptoms: F10 displayed on oven control panel
Oven shuts off during baking or self-clean
Oven may be extremely hot when error occurs

fixSteps.title: Cancel and cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Press Cancel to stop the alarm. If the oven does not respond, turn off the circuit breaker. Allow the oven to cool completely before any inspection.

fixSteps.title: Test oven temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the sensor at the back wall of the oven interior (two screws). Disconnect the sensor connector at the back of the range. Measure resistance: should be approximately 1080 ohms at room temperature (72F).

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring from the probe to the EOC board. Look for damaged insulation, pinched wires, or shorts to the oven frame.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor or EOC board
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor reads significantly off from 1080 ohms at room temperature, replace it. If the sensor is good but F10 persists, replace the electronic oven control (EOC) board.

whenToCallPro: If the temperature sensor tests normal and F10 persists, the EOC board likely needs replacement. Also call a pro if the oven relay is suspected of being welded shut.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F10 error temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Is F10 a fire hazard?
faq.a: F10 is a safety feature that prevents overheating. If the relay is actually stuck, the oven could overheat. Turn off the breaker until repaired.

faq.q: What resistance should the sensor read?
faq.a: At room temperature (72F/22C), the RTD sensor should read approximately 1080-1090 ohms. At 350F it reads about 1540 ohms.

faq.q: Can I use the stovetop burners with F10?
faq.a: On most models, the stovetop burners are independent of the oven control. They should still work. However, if you suspect a relay issue, use the breaker to power off everything until repaired.

title: Shorted Key on Control Panel
shortDescription: A key on the touchpad/control panel is stuck or shorted.
fullDescription: Error code F11 indicates the electronic oven control has detected that one or more buttons on the touchpad are stuck in a pressed or shorted state for longer than the expected debounce time. The control locks out to prevent unintended oven activation.

causes.cause: Moisture or food residue under touchpad
EOC board touchpad input circuit failure

symptoms: F11 error on display with beeping
Cannot clear the error by pressing Cancel

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven circuit breaker to stop the beeping and prevent any unintended heating. Wait 5 minutes and restore power.

fixSteps.title: Clean the touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: With the oven off, clean the entire touchpad surface with a damp cloth and mild cleaner. Remove any food residue or moisture around buttons.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck buttons
fixSteps.instruction: Press each button individually and feel for ones that do not spring back. A stuck button will cause continuous F11 errors.

fixSteps.title: Replace touchpad or EOC
fixSteps.instruction: If the touchpad is damaged, replace it. On many Frigidaire models, the touchpad and EOC are an integrated unit and must be replaced together.

whenToCallPro: If the oven turns on by itself and you cannot stop it with the breaker, call a professional immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F11 error stuck key touchpad fix

faq.q: My oven turned on by itself with F11. Is this dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, an oven that activates on its own is a fire hazard. Turn off the circuit breaker immediately and do not use the oven until repaired.

faq.q: Are the touchpad and control board the same part?
faq.a: On many Frigidaire models, they are a combined unit. Some models have a separate touchpad overlay that can be replaced independently.

faq.q: Can steam cleaning cause F11?
faq.a: Yes, steam from cooking or self-cleaning can condense behind the touchpad and cause temporary short circuits between button contacts.

title: Internal EOC Board Failure
shortDescription: The electronic oven control board has detected an internal hardware fault.
fullDescription: Error code F13 indicates the electronic oven control (EOC) board has detected an internal failure in its processor, memory, or output circuits. The board cannot safely control the oven and shuts down.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes. Restore power and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel or control panel cover. Reseat all wire harness connectors on the EOC board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burnt components, swollen capacitors, or discolored areas on the EOC board.

fixSteps.title: Replace EOC board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the electronic oven control board. Transfer any model-specific settings or overlays from the old board.

whenToCallPro: EOC replacement is straightforward, but if you are not comfortable working behind the control panel, a professional can help.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F13 error control board fix

faq.q: Is the EOC board expensive?
faq.a: EOC boards for Frigidaire ranges typically cost $150-$350 depending on the model and features.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F13?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges are a common cause of EOC board failure. A whole-house surge protector can help prevent this.

faq.q: Can the stovetop still work with F13?
faq.a: On some models with independent burner controls, the stovetop may still function. On models where the EOC controls everything, all functions may be disabled.

title: EOC Software/Calibration Error
shortDescription: The electronic oven control board has a software or calibration error.
fullDescription: Error code F14 indicates the EOC board's internal software or temperature calibration data is corrupted. The oven cannot accurately control temperature and shuts down for safety.
symptoms: Oven will not accept temperature settings

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 15 minutes. Restore power. The board may reinitialize its calibration data.

fixSteps.title: Verify EOC part number
fixSteps.instruction: If the board was recently replaced, confirm the part number is correct for your specific model.

fixSteps.title: Attempt factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Some models allow a factory reset through a specific button combination. Check the tech sheet for your model.

fixSteps.title: Replace EOC board
fixSteps.instruction: If the calibration data cannot be restored, replace the EOC board with the correct part number.

whenToCallPro: If a power cycle does not clear F14, the EOC board must be replaced.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F14 error calibration fix

faq.q: What is calibration data?
faq.a: Calibration data is factory-set information that tells the EOC board how to accurately interpret the temperature sensor readings for your specific oven model.

faq.q: Can I recalibrate the oven myself?
faq.a: Normal oven temperature calibration (adjusting by a few degrees) is available through the oven settings. But F14 indicates deeper data corruption that user calibration cannot fix.

faq.q: Is F14 related to temperature accuracy?
faq.a: Yes. Without valid calibration data, the oven cannot maintain accurate temperatures and disables itself for safety.

title: Oven Temperature Sensor Open/Shorted
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor circuit is open or shorted.
fullDescription: Error code F30 indicates the EOC board has detected that the oven temperature sensor (RTD) circuit is either open (disconnected) or shorted. Without a valid temperature reading, the oven cannot safely control heating.
symptoms: Error appears when trying to start bake or broil

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Access the sensor connector at the back of the range. Ensure the plug is firmly connected. It may have been knocked loose during cleaning.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor from the EOC. Measure resistance across the sensor leads. At room temperature, it should read approximately 1080 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring continuity
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests good at the probe end, test continuity through the wiring to the EOC connector. A broken wire in the harness will cause F30.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the two mounting screws inside the oven. Pull the sensor out through the back wall. Install the replacement and connect.

whenToCallPro: Only if the sensor and wiring test good but F30 persists, indicating an EOC board input failure.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F30 error temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Where is the temperature sensor?
faq.a: The sensor is a metal probe extending from the back wall of the oven interior, usually near the top. It is held in by two screws.

faq.q: Can I test the sensor without removing it?
faq.a: Yes. Disconnect the sensor plug at the back of the range and measure resistance across the two pins. No need to remove from the oven.

faq.q: Is F30 the same as F10?
faq.a: No. F30 means the sensor circuit is open or shorted (electrical problem). F10 means the temperature reading is too high (temperature problem). However, a shorted sensor can cause either code.

title: Oven Temperature Sensor Shorted
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor has a short circuit, reading abnormally low resistance.
fullDescription: Error code F31 specifically indicates the oven temperature sensor (RTD) circuit resistance is too low, suggesting a short circuit. The sensor resistance is well below the expected range for any normal temperature. The EOC board cannot determine the actual oven temperature.
symptoms: Error may appear during self-clean (high temp damages weakened sensor)

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. A shorted sensor will read very low (near 0 ohms) instead of the expected 1080 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Check the sensor connector for moisture or corrosion. Dry and clean with contact cleaner. Moisture between the pins can cause a short.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring for shorts
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the sensor wiring for areas where insulation may be damaged and the two wires are touching each other or touching the oven frame.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the temperature sensor. Route the new sensor wire carefully to avoid pinching against hot surfaces.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not clear F31, the EOC board may have an internal short on the sensor input.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F31 error sensor shorted fix

faq.q: Why did the sensor short?
faq.a: Repeated heating and cooling cycles can eventually break down the sensor's internal insulation, causing the resistance element to short circuit.

faq.q: Can self-clean cause F31?
faq.a: Yes. The extreme temperatures during self-clean (900F+) can push a weakening sensor past its failure point, causing it to short.

faq.q: Is the sensor cheap to replace?
faq.a: Yes. Oven temperature sensors are one of the least expensive oven parts, typically $10-$30, and are easy to install.

title: Door Lock Motor Circuit Failure
shortDescription: The oven door lock motor circuit has failed - the door cannot lock for self-clean.
fullDescription: Error code F90 indicates a failure in the oven door lock motor circuit. The door lock mechanism is used during self-clean cycles to keep the door securely closed at extremely high temperatures. The lock motor is not responding to the control board's commands.
symptoms: Door does not lock for self-clean
Clicking or grinding from lock area

fixSteps.title: Check door lock manually
fixSteps.instruction: Some Frigidaire ovens have a manual lock lever. Try manually engaging and disengaging the lock to check for mechanical binding.

fixSteps.title: Inspect lock motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Access the door lock motor (typically behind the control panel or above the oven door). Check the wiring connector.

fixSteps.title: Test lock motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the lock motor wiring. Apply 120V AC directly to the motor to test if it operates. If it does not, the motor is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screws for the door lock assembly. Disconnect the wiring. Install the new assembly and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable testing with live voltage, or if the EOC lock circuit is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F90 error door lock motor fix

faq.q: Can I still bake with F90?
faq.a: Yes, regular baking does not require the door lock. F90 only prevents self-clean cycles.

faq.q: Can I manually lock the door for self-clean?
faq.a: Some models have a manual lock lever that can be used even if the motor fails. However, ensure the lock switch is also functioning.

faq.q: Why does the door need to lock for self-clean?
faq.a: Self-clean cycles reach 900F+ which is dangerously hot. The door lock prevents accidental opening during this extreme heat.

title: Door Unlock Failure
shortDescription: The oven door failed to unlock after a self-clean cycle.
fullDescription: Error code F91 indicates the oven door lock mechanism did not unlock after the self-clean cycle completed and the oven cooled down. The door remains locked and the control board could not confirm the door has returned to the unlocked position.

causes.cause: Lock motor stuck in locked position
Oven still too hot to unlock

symptoms: Cannot open door to access food or oven interior
Lock motor may hum or click without unlocking

fixSteps.title: Wait for cooling
fixSteps.instruction: The door will not unlock until the oven temperature drops below approximately 550F. Wait 30-60 minutes after self-clean ends for the oven to cool sufficiently.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power. The lock motor may reset and attempt to unlock.

fixSteps.title: Manual unlock
fixSteps.instruction: If the door remains locked, access the lock mechanism from behind the control panel. Some models have a release lever or the lock rod can be manually repositioned.

fixSteps.title: Replace lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the lock mechanism is jammed or the motor has failed, replace the entire door lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot manually release the door lock, a technician can safely access the mechanism.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F91 error door stuck locked fix

faq.q: How long does the oven take to cool after self-clean?
faq.a: It can take 30-90 minutes after the self-clean cycle ends for the oven to cool enough for the door to unlock, depending on your model.

faq.q: Can I force the door open?
faq.a: Do not force the door. You can damage the lock mechanism and door hinges. Use the manual release or wait for the oven to cool.

faq.q: Is my food trapped inside?
faq.a: You should never leave food in the oven during self-clean. The 900F+ temperatures will incinerate anything inside. If food is trapped due to an accidental self-clean start, turn off the breaker and wait for cooling.

title: Door Lock Wiring Harness Open/Shorted
shortDescription: The wiring to the door lock assembly has an open or short circuit.
fullDescription: Error code F92 indicates the EOC board detected an electrical fault in the wiring between the control board and the door lock assembly. The circuit is either open (broken wire) or shorted.

causes.cause: Broken or pinched door lock wiring
Loose wire connector at lock or control board
Heat damage to wiring near oven cavity

symptoms: Door lock does not respond at all
No motor sound from lock area

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Access the door lock connector and the EOC board. Reseat all connectors related to the door lock circuit.

fixSteps.title: Test wiring continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the lock assembly wiring at both ends. Test continuity of each wire in the harness. An open wire needs to be repaired or the harness replaced.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for heat damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the lock wiring where it passes near the oven cavity for melted insulation or heat damage.

fixSteps.title: Repair or replace wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Repair broken wires with high-temperature wire and connectors. If damage is extensive, replace the wire harness.

whenToCallPro: If the wiring damage is extensive or you cannot access the harness routing.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F92 error door lock wiring fix

faq.q: Can I bake normally with F92?
faq.a: Yes, F92 only affects the self-clean function. Regular baking, broiling, and other oven functions should work normally.

faq.q: Why does wiring get damaged?
faq.a: The high temperatures inside and around the oven can degrade wire insulation over time, especially if wires are routed too close to the oven cavity.

faq.q: Should I use regular wire for repairs?
faq.a: No. Use high-temperature rated wire and connectors for any repairs near the oven. Standard wire insulation will melt.

title: Door Lock Incomplete
shortDescription: The door lock mechanism did not fully engage within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error code F93 indicates the door lock motor was energized but the lock position switch did not confirm full lock engagement within the timeout period. The lock may be partially engaged or the position sensor is faulty.

causes.cause: Mechanical obstruction preventing full lock engagement

symptoms: F93 error when self-clean tries to start
Lock motor runs but door does not fully lock

fixSteps.title: Clear obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the oven door and check around the lock mechanism area for food debris, spills, or anything that could prevent the lock from engaging.

fixSteps.title: Check door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door and check for even gaps around the perimeter. A misaligned door may not position the lock strike correctly. Adjust hinges if needed.

fixSteps.title: Lubricate lock mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Apply a small amount of high-temperature lubricant to the lock mechanism slide. Do not use WD-40 or standard grease as they will burn off.

fixSteps.title: Replace lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the mechanism is worn or the position switch is faulty, replace the complete door lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the lock mechanism is severely worn or the door itself needs realignment.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F93 error door lock incomplete fix

faq.q: Is F93 serious?
faq.a: F93 is not a safety emergency since the oven stops the self-clean from starting. Regular cooking functions are not affected.

faq.q: Can spills cause F93?
faq.a: Yes. Baked-on food spills around the lock area can physically block the lock from fully engaging.

faq.q: Does the oven door need to be replaced?
faq.a: Usually no. F93 is typically caused by the lock mechanism, not the door itself. The lock assembly is a separate replaceable part.

title: Door Lock Overheated
shortDescription: The door lock motor has overheated from prolonged operation.
fullDescription: Error code F94 indicates the door lock motor has been running for too long during repeated lock/unlock attempts, causing it to overheat. The EOC board shuts down the lock motor to prevent damage.

causes.cause: Repeated failed lock attempts causing motor overheating
EOC continuously cycling lock motor due to faulty switch

symptoms: F94 after multiple self-clean start attempts
Door lock motor hot to touch
Previous F90 or F93 errors before F94

fixSteps.title: Allow cooling
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker and let the lock motor cool for at least 30 minutes before any further operation.

fixSteps.title: Resolve underlying lock issue
fixSteps.instruction: Before attempting self-clean again, fix whatever prevented the lock from engaging (F90 or F93 issues). Clean and lubricate the mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Check lock switch alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the lock position switch activates properly when the lock is fully engaged. A misaligned switch will cause the motor to run continuously.

fixSteps.title: Replace lock motor if damaged
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor overheated severely, it may be permanently damaged. Replace the door lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot identify why the lock is not engaging, to prevent further motor overheating damage.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F94 error lock motor overheated fix

faq.q: Can an overheated motor be permanently damaged?
faq.a: Yes. If the motor windings got hot enough, the insulation can melt and the motor will fail permanently.

faq.q: How many lock attempts cause F94?
faq.a: Typically 3-5 consecutive failed lock attempts within a short period will cause the motor to overheat and trigger F94.

faq.q: Should I avoid self-clean until fixed?
faq.a: Yes. Do not attempt self-clean until the underlying lock issue is resolved, or you risk further damaging the lock motor.

title: Door Lock Position Switch Error
shortDescription: The door lock position switch is providing contradictory or invalid readings.
fullDescription: Error code F95 indicates the door lock position switches are providing contradictory information to the EOC board. For example, both the locked and unlocked switches may be active simultaneously, or neither may be active.

causes.cause: Broken switch actuator in lock mechanism
Wiring fault between switch and EOC

symptoms: Door lock status unknown to control board
May appear after power outage during self-clean

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. This may allow the lock mechanism to settle into a defined position.

fixSteps.title: Manually position lock
fixSteps.instruction: Access the lock mechanism and manually move it to a fully locked or unlocked position. This may resolve the contradictory switch state.

fixSteps.title: Test position switches
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test each lock position switch independently. Each should have a clear open/closed state depending on lock position.

fixSteps.title: Replace lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the switches are faulty, replace the complete door lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the lock assembly replacement does not resolve F95, the EOC board may have an input circuit failure.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire oven F95 error lock switch position fix

faq.q: Why did F95 appear after a power outage?
faq.a: If power was lost during a self-clean cycle while the lock was transitioning, it may be stuck in an intermediate position, causing conflicting switch readings.

faq.q: Can I bake with F95?
faq.a: Usually yes. The lock system is primarily for self-clean. However, if the control board cannot determine door state, some functions may be limited.

faq.q: What are lock position switches?
faq.a: These are micro-switches inside the lock assembly that tell the control board whether the door is fully locked, fully unlocked, or in transition.
